steps.title: Contrast flexibility and weight
steps.content: The first sentence says the jumpsuit material was flexible enough for Elvis Presley's dance moves. The next sentence says the embroidered feathers' added weight likely limited his mobility. The transition must signal contrast.

steps.title: Reject noncontrast transitions
steps.content: Choice B starts a list, Choice C restates an idea, and Choice D signals cause and effect. The second sentence complicates the first by introducing an opposing limitation. The answer is A.
